steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
You are a log analyst investigating access patterns on a web server. There is a directory at /home/user/server_logs containing several Apache-style access log files from different dates.

Your task is to find all log entries across all .log files in the /home/user/server_logs directory that contain HTTP 404 error responses and save them to a single consolidated file.

Specifically:
1. Search through all files ending in .log within /home/user/server_logs
2. Find all lines that contain the pattern " 404 " (the HTTP status code 404 surrounded by spaces)
3. Save all matching lines to a new file at /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt

The output file should contain the raw matching lines exactly as they appear in the original log files, with each match on its own line. The file should be created even if no matches are found (in which case it would be empty).

Make sure the /home/user/analysis directory exists before writing the output file.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user#

Analysis: I'm currently in /home/user directory as root. The task requires me to:
1. Search all .log files in /home/user/server_logs for lines containing ' 404 '
2. Save matching lines to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
3. Ensure the analysis directory exists before writing the file

I should first explore the directory structure to understand what I'm working with.
Plan: I'll start by:
1. Checking if the server_logs directory exists and what files it contains
2. Creating the analysis directory if it doesn't exist
3. Using grep to search for ' 404 ' pattern in all .log files
4. Redirecting the output to the target file

I'll use 'grep -h' to search without printing filenames, or just 'grep' to include them. Since the task asks for 'raw matching lines exactly as they appear', I'll use 'grep -h' to avoid filename prefixes.

results.content:
Previous response had warnings:
WARNINGS: - Extra text detected before JSON object

total 12

drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Apr 17 17:57 ..
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Apr 17 18:27 server_logs
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# ls -la /home/user/server_logs/
total 24
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Apr 17 18:27 .
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Apr 17 18:27 ..
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root  340 Apr 17 18:27 access_2024_01_15.log
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root  313 Apr 17 18:27 access_2024_01_16.log
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root  247 Apr 17 18:27 access_2024_01_17.log
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root   37 Apr 17 18:27 notes.txt
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user#

steps.message:
Analysis: Good! I can see:
- The /home/user/server_logs directory exists and contains 3 .log files (access_2024_01_15.log, access_2024_01_16.log, access_2024_01_17.log) and a notes.txt file
- The /home/user/analysis directory does not exist yet and needs to be created
- I need to search through the .log files for lines containing ' 404 ' and save them to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
Plan: Next steps:
1. First, let me peek at one of the log files to understand the format
2. Create the /home/user/analysis directory
3. Use grep to search all .log files for the pattern ' 404 ' (space-404-space)
4. Redirect the output to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
5. Verify the output file was created and contains the expected results

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# head -5 /home/user/server_logs/access_2024_01_15.log
192.168.1.100 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:15:32 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1234
192.168.1.101 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:16:45 +0000] "GET /missing-page.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
192.168.1.102 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:17:22 +0000] "GET /about.html HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
192.168.1.103 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:18:01 +0000] "GET /old-link.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# mkdir -p /home/user/analysis
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# grep -h ' 404 ' /home/user/server_logs/*.log > /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# ls -la /home/user/analysis/
total 12
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr 20 21:00 .
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Apr 20 21:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  339 Apr 20 21:00 404_errors.txt
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user# cat /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
192.168.1.101 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:16:45 +0000] "GET /missing-page.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
192.168.1.103 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:18:01 +0000] "GET /old-link.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
10.0.0.51 - - [16/Jan/2024:08:31:22 +0000] "GET /nonexistent HTTP/1.1" 404 512
172.16.0.26 - - [17/Jan/2024:14:21:30 +0000] "GET /deleted-resource HTTP/1.1" 404 512
root@c730a6bfbdf7:/home/user#

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! The task has been completed successfully:
- The /home/user/analysis directory was created
- All .log files in /home/user/server_logs were searched for lines containing ' 404 '
- Found 4 log entries with 404 errors across the three log files
- All matching lines were saved to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
- The output file contains the raw matching lines exactly as they appear in the original log files

The 404_errors.txt file contains:
1. Two entries from access_2024_01_15.log
2. One entry from access_2024_01_16.log
3. One entry from access_2024_01_17.log

All entries show HTTP 404 error responses with the complete Apache log format preserved.
Plan: The task is complete. All requirements have been met:
1. ✓ Searched through all .log files in /home/user/server_logs
2. ✓ Found all lines containing ' 404 ' pattern
3. ✓ Saved matching lines to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
4. ✓ Created the analysis directory before writing the file
5. ✓ Output file contains raw matching lines exactly as they appear in original files
